summary: MongoDB-oriented persistence recommendation for the locker booking API contract.

identifierPolicy.publicId: Use UUID/string id fields for every client-facing resource.
identifierPolicy.internalId: MongoDB _id/ObjectId may exist internally but must never be returned in API responses.
identifierPolicy.foreignKeys: Store userId, locationId, lockerId, and bookingId as public UUID/string values or maintain mapped internal references behind repository boundaries.

collections.name: users
collections.purpose: OAuth identity, role, profile, and provider links.

collections.name: refreshTokens
collections.purpose: Refresh sessions, token rotation, revocation, and device/session metadata.

collections.name: lockers
collections.purpose: Permanent locker inventory and operational status.

collections.name: bookings
collections.purpose: Booking lifecycle. Occupancy is derived from ACTIVE bookings and current UTC time.

collections.name: lockerAccessLogs
collections.purpose: Append-only open/close audit stream and 3 minute open throttle lookup.

bookingOverlapOptimization.transactionRecommendation: Use a MongoDB transaction or equivalent atomic reservation workflow for create booking, with a retry strategy around write conflicts.
bookingOverlapOptimization.timePolicy: Use server-side UTC time only. Treat booking interval as half-open: [startAt, endAt).
bookingOverlapOptimization.statusPolicy: COMPLETED, CANCELLED, and EXPIRED bookings do not block new bookings.

accessLogOptimization.openThrottleLookup: Find latest allowed OPEN log by bookingId, lockerId, action OPEN, sorted requestedAt descending. Reject if requestedAt is within 3 minutes.
accessLogOptimization.auditRetention: Use TTL only if retention policy allows it; otherwise archive to cold storage by time partition.
